description_long: This helmet features a rounded dome constructed from brown, textured material resembling leather, reinforced with metallic bands and rivets that segment the top. The front faceplate is a pale, bone-like material with two rectangular eye openings and a pointed lower edge, providing both protection and visibility. The sides and rear are fully enclosed, with the same brown, leather-like texture extending downward to cover the neck and sides of the head. The metallic bands are silver-gray and add structural integrity, while the rivets provide a rugged, medieval appearance. There are no visible ventilation holes, logos, or graphic designs, giving the helmet a utilitarian and historical look. The interior is not visible, but it is likely hollow to accommodate a human head.
description: A brown, leather-textured dome helmet with silver metallic bands and a pale, angular faceplate featuring eye slits. The design is fully enclosed and medieval in style, with no visible graphics or ventilation.
description_view_0: Front view shows the pale, angular faceplate with two rectangular eye openings and the segmented dome top.
description_view_1: Left rear view highlights the continuous brown leather texture and the metallic banding, with the side of the faceplate partially visible.
description_view_2: Rear view displays the uninterrupted brown, leather-like surface and the full coverage of the helmet's back.
description_view_3: Right rear view shows the side of the faceplate and the helmet's downward extension, with metallic and leather textures visible.
